sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: SJB ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:SJB
feed.summary: The ProShares Short High Yield (SJB) ETF is designed to deliver the inverse daily returns of a high-yield USD-denominated bond index, making it a short-term trading tool rather than a buy-and-hold investment. It has assets under management of $126.83M, an expense ratio of 0.95%, and pays quarterly dividends with a yield of 3.79%. SJB trades at a premium of 0.08% and has recently seen significant fund inflows.

feed.title: BBSI (BBSI) EVP James Potts sells 1,803 shares in pre-set 10b5-1 trade
feed.summary: James R. Potts, EVP, General Counsel & Secretary of BARRETT BUSINESS SERVICES INC (BBSI), sold 1,803 shares of common stock at $28.68 per share. This transaction was conducted under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted on December 15, 2025, meaning it was pre-scheduled. Following the sale, Potts still directly owns 33,837 shares of BBSI.

feed.title: Insider Sell Alert: Andrew Wiechmann Sells Shares of MSCI Inc (M
feed.summary: Andrew Wiechmann, CFO of an MSCI Inc subsidiary, recently sold 450 shares of MSCI Inc, reducing his total holdings to 22,544 shares. This transaction is part of a larger trend of insider selling at the company, with 6 insider sells against 4 insider buys over the past year. Despite the insider selling, MSCI Inc's stock is currently considered modestly undervalued based on its GuruFocus Value.

feed.title: Insider Sell: Dario Baroni Sells 600 Shares of McDonald's Corp (MCD)
feed.summary: Dario Baroni, President of IDL, sold 600 shares of McDonald's Corp (MCD) on March 18, 2026, reducing his holdings to 1,063.07 shares. The transaction report indicates 25 insider sells and no insider buys in the past year. McDonald's stock was trading at $323.77, with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.01, suggesting it is fairly valued.

feed.title: Rhythm Pharmaceuticals Announces FDA Approval of IMCIVREE®
feed.summary: Rhythm Pharmaceuticals announced that the FDA has approved an expanded indication for IMCIVREE® (setmelanotide) to treat patients aged 4 years and older with acquired hypothalamic obesity (HO). This makes IMCIVREE the first and only FDA-approved therapy for this rare disease, which is characterized by accelerated and sustained weight gain due to hypothalamic injury. The approval is supported by positive data from the Phase 3 TRANSCEND trial, showing a significant reduction in BMI.

feed.title: Capital One buys $125M property in Tysons
feed.summary: An entity associated with Capital One Financial Corp. has acquired multiple properties in Tysons for $125 million, directly across from the bank's headquarters. The purchased properties, formerly owned primarily by entities connected to Cityline Partners, include office buildings and land parcels. This acquisition follows a previous purchase in 2019 by a Capital One affiliate in the same area.

feed.title: Is It Time To Revisit General Electric (GE) After Its Recent Share Price Pullback?
feed.summary: General Electric (GE) has experienced a recent stock price pullback, declining 4.9% over 7 days and 10.8% over 30 days, although it has seen significant multi-year returns. An analysis using a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model suggests GE is approximately 7.1% overvalued at US$291.61, indicating it is fairly valued. However, its Price-to-Earnings (P/E) ratio of 35.42x is below its peer and industry averages, and Simply Wall St's "Fair Ratio" calculation suggests the stock is undervalued based on earnings.

feed.title: How Record Backlog and Higher 2026 EPS Outlook Could Shape Quanta Services (PWR) Investors
feed.summary: Quanta Services (PWR) reported strong Q4 2025 results, surpassing Wall Street expectations and raising its 2026 adjusted EPS outlook to $12.65–$13.35. The company also disclosed a record backlog of nearly $44.00 billion, driven by significant utility, renewable energy, and data center infrastructure projects. This substantial backlog and positive earnings forecast reinforce Quanta's role as a key contractor in long-duration energy transition and digital infrastructure, although it also highlights a dependency on sustained capital spending and permitting trends.

feed.title: A Look At Labcorp Holdings (LH) Valuation After Expanded Illumina Precision Oncology Alliance
feed.summary: Labcorp Holdings (LH) has expanded its collaboration with Illumina to enhance access to next-generation sequencing for precision oncology tests. Despite a 1-year total shareholder return of 13.21%, the stock trades at US$264.09, which Simply Wall St identifies as undervalued by approximately 14.2% with a fair value of $307.71, based on anticipated revenue growth from new tests and improved margins through technology. However, potential risks like tariffs and increased competition could affect this valuation.

feed.title: Adaire Fox-Martin: Global Infrastructure and Digital Leader, Equinix President and CEO, and Champion of Interconnected, Sustainable Data Centers
feed.summary: Adaire Fox-Martin is the President and CEO of Equinix, a global digital infrastructure company. She has led Equinix through significant expansion, focusing on AI and cloud workloads, sustainability, and global presence. Her career journey includes leadership roles at SAP, eBay, Meta, Instacart, and OpenAI, demonstrating resilience and strategic vision in the technology sector.

feed.title: Why Japanese Asset Owners Should Rethink Their Currency Hedge
feed.summary: This article examines how the global investment landscape, particularly the outsized influence of U.S. equities and the yen's depreciation, has reshaped portfolio risk for Japanese asset owners. It analyzes how currency hedging impacts risk, return, and diversification, highlighting that the USD's contribution to currency risk has significantly increased. The research suggests that while hedging can reduce volatility, there is no universally optimal hedge ratio, and active currency management is crucial for long-term portfolio resilience.

feed.title: B. Riley Raises Price Target on Hyperfine to $1.60 From $1.30, Keeps Buy Rating
feed.summary: B. Riley has increased its price target for Hyperfine, Inc. (NASDAQ: HYPR) to $1.60 from $1.30, while maintaining a Buy rating on the stock. This adjustment follows recent company news, including Hyperfine's Q4 2025 earnings call and a new loan and security agreement. Hyperfine, a health technology company, develops and markets the portable Swoop Portable Magnetic Resonance Imaging System.

feed.title: Rocatinlimab Halt Puts Spotlight On Amgen Pipeline Risks And Valuation
feed.summary: Amgen and Kyowa Kirin have ceased all clinical trials for rocatinlimab due to newly identified safety concerns, including cases of malignancy. This decision removes a significant immunology asset from Amgen's pipeline, raising questions about the company's R&D strategy and risk profile for late-stage assets. Despite this setback, Amgen's stock is currently trading near analyst targets and is flagged as undervalued by Simply Wall St, although it has experienced recent negative momentum.

feed.title: VNQI vs. REET: Which Global Real Estate ETF Is the Best Fit for Your Portfolio?
feed.summary: This article compares two global real estate ETFs, Vanguard Global ex-U.S. Real Estate ETF (VNQI) and iShares Global REIT ETF (REET), to help investors choose the best fit. VNQI offers a higher dividend yield and broader international diversification but comes with increased volatility, while REET focuses more on U.S.-listed real estate names. The choice depends on an investor's preference for income vs. growth, regional diversification, and comfort with risk.

feed.title: Invesco QQQ ETF
feed.summary: The Invesco QQQ ETF (NASDAQ: QQQ) tracks the Nasdaq-100 Index, offering exposure to the 100 largest non-financial companies on the Nasdaq, including tech giants like Apple and Amazon. It is known for its focus on innovation and technology, high liquidity, and a low annual expense ratio of 0.20%. The article provides a summary, key features, and performance data for the ETF.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Industrial Goods Companies: Equifax (EFX) and Boeing (BA)
feed.summary: Analysts from Needham and Jefferies have issued optimistic reports for Equifax (EFX) and Boeing (BA) respectively. Needham maintained a Buy rating for Equifax with a significant upside, while Jefferies reiterated a Buy rating for Boeing, also indicating substantial potential growth. Both companies received "Buy" or "Strong Buy" consensus ratings from analysts across the financial sector.

feed.title: Skye Bioscience Faces Nasdaq Listing Compliance Deficiency Notice
feed.summary: Skye Bioscience (SKYE) has received a Nasdaq deficiency notice because its common stock traded below the $1.00 minimum bid price for 30 consecutive business days, risking its Nasdaq Global Market listing. The company has until September 14, 2026, to regain compliance, possibly through a reverse stock split. Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, rates SKYE as Neutral due to weak financial performance and bearish technical signals, despite some positive offsets from recent earnings calls and Phase 2a data.

feed.title: McKesson Corporation opens registration for ideaShare 2026 to unite pharmacy professionals
feed.summary: McKesson Corporation has opened registration for its annual ideaShare 2026 event, aiming to strengthen the independent pharmacy sector through networking and innovation. This initiative builds on previous strategic moves like a patient hub model for cell and gene therapies and funding for state pharmacy associations, demonstrating McKesson's commitment to advancing patient care and pharmacy business resilience. The event seeks to offer crucial resources and opportunities for pharmacy professionals.

feed.title: Bristol-Myers Squibb Company stock faces pipeline promise amid patent cliffs and Q1 earnings anticip
feed.summary: Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMS) stock is under investor scrutiny as new clinical trial successes for mezigdomide and FDA approval for Sotyktu are pitted against significant patent cliffs and declining legacy drug revenues. The company's strategic shift toward targeted therapies and immunology is crucial for navigating U.S. biotech patent transitions. Investors, particularly in German-speaking regions, are balancing the high dividend yield and pipeline potential against concerns about revenue forecasts, debt levels, and execution risks ahead of Q1 2026 earnings.

feed.title: Nio's in-house chip production exceeds 550,000 units, says William Li
feed.summary: Nio's cumulative in-house chip production has surpassed 550,000 units, significantly driven by their Yangjian LiDAR chip and Shenji NX9031 smart driving chip. This initiative has led to substantial cost savings per vehicle and improved performance, validating Nio's strategy of investing in R&D for higher long-term gross margins. The company aims to standardize automotive chips and expects 35-40% of its automotive semiconductors to be domestically sourced by 2027.

feed.title: News | Prologis forms $1.6 billion venture to bet on made-to-order logistics properties
feed.summary: Prologis has partnered with Singapore's sovereign wealth fund GIC in a $1.6 billion joint venture to develop and acquire 4.1 million square feet of build-to-suit logistics facilities across the U.S. This collaboration signals a potential rebound in the U.S. industrial property market, with a focus on mitigating risk by developing properties for specific tenants like Amazon. The venture aims to capitalize on strong customer conviction for made-to-order logistics spaces as speculative construction slows due to market conditions.

feed.title: Analysts Offer Insights on Industrial Goods Companies: Equifax (EFX) and Boeing (BA)
feed.summary: This article highlights bullish sentiments from analysts regarding Equifax (EFX) and Boeing (BA) in the Industrial Goods sector. Kyle Peterson from Needham maintained a Buy rating on Equifax, anticipating a 25.4% upside, while Sheila Kahyaoglu from Jefferies maintained a Buy rating on Boeing, expecting a 32.5% upside. Both companies show strong analyst consensus for future growth.

feed.title: Insider Buying: Reynolds Consumer Products (NASDAQ:REYN) Director Purchases 4,705 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Reynolds Consumer Products (NASDAQ:REYN) Director Rolf Stangl recently purchased 4,705 shares of the company's stock, increasing his direct ownership by 13.51%. The shares were acquired at an average price of $21.06 per share, totaling approximately $99,087. This insider buying activity comes as the company trades near its 52-week lows, with a current dividend yield of 4.4% and analysts maintaining a "Hold" rating with a consensus price target of $25.43.

feed.title: Weekly Recalls: Audi, Ford (2), General Motors, Kia, Jeep, Volvo
feed.summary: This article details the latest weekly vehicle recalls from several major manufacturers, including Audi, Ford, General Motors, Kia, Jeep, and Volvo. The recalls address various safety concerns such as lane departure system errors, headlight malfunctions, potential loss of drive power, electronic owner's manual download issues, liftgate cover detachment, seat belt buckle problems, and seat belt warning system failures. Owners are provided with remedy information, notification dates, and customer service contacts for each specific recall.

feed.title: Verisk Analytics (NASDAQ:VRSK) CFO Sells $81,884.00 in Stock
feed.summary: Verisk Analytics CFO Elizabeth Mann sold 400 shares of the company's stock for $81,884.00 on March 17th, reducing her ownership by 1.92%. The sale follows Verisk beating quarterly earnings expectations and announcing a $2.5 billion stock buyback program and an increased quarterly dividend. Analysts currently rate Verisk Analytics as a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of $237.20.

feed.title: SRB Corp sells $87,599 in Safety Insurance Group (SAFT) shares
feed.summary: SRB Corp, a ten percent owner of Safety Insurance Group (SAFT), sold 1,199 shares totaling $87,599 on March 17, 2026. Following this transaction, SRB Corp. and Plymouth Rock Co Inc jointly own 1,808,806 shares of Safety Insurance Group. The company also declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.92 per share and plans to release its Q4 and full-year 2025 financial results on February 25, 2026.

feed.title: Skye Bioscience receives Nasdaq notice for minimum bid price deficiency
feed.summary: Skye Bioscience, Inc. (SKYE) has received a deficiency letter from Nasdaq for failing to meet the minimum $1.00 per share bid price requirement for 30 consecutive business days. The company has 180 calendar days, until September 14, 2026, to regain compliance by having its stock close at or above $1.00 for at least 10 consecutive business days, and it may consider a reverse stock split as an option. Skye Bioscience intends to monitor its stock price and explore options to resolve the deficiency, though there is no guarantee of compliance.

feed.title: FedEx raises guidance again after strong earnings beat
feed.summary: FedEx (NYSE: FDX) reported strong fiscal third-quarter earnings, exceeding analyst expectations, and consequently raised its full-year guidance. The company's revenue increased 8% year-over-year to $24 billion, and adjusted earnings per share rose 16% to $5.25, driven by strong yield and volume across package services and cost savings. FedEx now anticipates fiscal year 2026 revenue to grow 6.25% at the mid-range and adjusted EPS of $19.30 to $20.10.

feed.title: FedEx raises guidance again after strong earnings beat
feed.summary: FedEx reported strong fiscal third-quarter earnings, exceeding analyst expectations, and subsequently raised its full-year guidance for revenue and adjusted EPS. The company's stock price jumped significantly in aftermarket trading, reflecting investor confidence in its Network 2.0 integration and cost-saving initiatives. FedEx plans to continue closing parcel terminals and projects over $1 billion in transformation-related savings for FY26.

feed.title: Live Nation CEO hounded over Ticketmaster fees, ‘outrageous’ concert add-ons at antitrust trial
feed.summary: Live Nation CEO Michael Rapino faced intense questioning at an antitrust trial regarding Ticketmaster fees and "outrageous" concert add-ons. States are suing Live Nation, alleging an unlawful monopoly that leads to inflated prices and anti-competitive practices. Testimony revealed Rapino acknowledged high fees in 2016 and defended a controversial $15 chair rental fee, while denying accusations of pressuring venues or being aware of rising service fees.

feed.title: Applied Materials, Inc. stock faces valuation scrutiny amid AI boom and institutional shifts
feed.summary: Applied Materials, Inc. (AMAT) is under investor scrutiny due to institutional stake reductions and a strong Q1 2026 earnings beat. The article highlights the tension between AI-driven growth and high valuations in the semiconductor equipment sector. Despite robust earnings and crucial roles in next-gen tech, concerns persist regarding valuation, semiconductor cyclicality, and geopolitical risks, making it a nuanced investment for DACH portfolios.

feed.title: Earnings call transcript: Scholastic Q3 2026 beats EPS forecast, stock slips
feed.summary: Scholastic Corporation reported mixed results for Q3 fiscal year 2026, with EPS significantly beating forecasts at -$0.15 against an expected -$0.60, a 75% positive surprise. However, revenue of $329.1 million fell slightly short of the $331.53 million forecast. Despite the EPS beat, the stock declined by 4.16% in premarket trading, influenced by the revenue shortfall and broader market sentiment.

feed.title: AMETEK (NYSE: AME) CCO granted options, RSUs and exercises awards
feed.summary: AMETEK's Chief Commercial Officer, Emanuela Speranza, was granted stock options for 1,790 shares and 570 restricted stock units (RSUs), both vesting in three equal annual installments starting March 18, 2027. She also exercised 296 previously granted RSUs into common shares, with 134 shares withheld to cover taxes. Following these transactions, Speranza directly holds 30,201 common shares, 1,790 stock options, and 594 RSUs.

feed.title: Scholastic (SCHL) Reports Q3 Revenue Slightly Below Expectations
feed.summary: Scholastic (SCHL) reported third-quarter revenue of $329.1 million, slightly below expectations, but emphasized strategic execution and shareholder value creation. The company initiated a new $300 million share repurchase program, including a $200 million "modified Dutch auction tender offer," after having already returned over $147 million to shareholders. While profitability metrics show challenges, Scholastic maintains adequate liquidity and a robust financial position, aiming for growth in fiscal year 2027.

feed.title: Nasdaq warns Skye Bioscience (SKYE) after shares stay under $1 minimum
feed.summary: Skye Bioscience (SKYE) has received a deficiency letter from Nasdaq for failing to maintain a minimum bid price of $1.00 per share for 30 consecutive business days, violating Nasdaq Listing Rule 5450(a)(1). The company has 180 calendar days, until September 14, 2026, to regain compliance, potentially through a reverse stock split. Failure to comply could lead to delisting, though a second 180-day grace period on The Nasdaq Capital Market may be possible if other listing standards are met.

feed.title: Skye Bioscience Disclosed Failure to Satisfy a Continued Listing Rule or Standard
feed.summary: Skye Bioscience (SKYE) received a Nasdaq deficiency letter on March 17, 2026, for failing to maintain the minimum $1.00 bid price for 30 consecutive business days. The company has 180 days, until September 14, 2026, to regain compliance, with a reverse stock split as an option. SKYE's stock continues to trade on the Nasdaq Global Market.

feed.title: SFL Corp (SFL) director lists 35,000 share options on Form 3
feed.summary: SFL Corp Ltd. director Jan Erik Klepsland has filed an initial statement of beneficial ownership (Form 3) disclosing 35,000 share options. These options have an exercise price of $10.28 per share, expire on February 19, 2031, and will vest in three equal annual installments starting February 19, 2027. The filing reports existing option holdings and does not indicate any purchases or sales of shares.

feed.title: Santander Brasil (NYSE: BSBR) enters €44M 2026 intercompany services agreement
feed.summary: Santander Brasil (NYSE: BSBR) announced a €44 million intercompany services agreement for 2026 with its indirect controlling shareholder, Banco Santander S.A. in Spain. This agreement, linked to a 2023 master services contract, covers various strategic, technical, and operational intra-group services including risk management, technology, and regulatory support. Management states the deal was negotiated on an arm’s length, market-based basis and approved through established group committees.

feed.title: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP Filed a Securities Fraud Class Action Lawsuit Against uniQure N.V. (QURE); April 13, 2026, Lead Plaintiff Deadline – Company Announcement - FT.com
feed.summary: Kessler Topaz Meltzer & Check, LLP has filed a securities fraud class action lawsuit against uniQure N.V. (NASDAQ: QURE) on behalf of investors who purchased ordinary shares between September 24, 2025, and October 31, 2025. The lawsuit alleges that uniQure made material misstatements regarding its Huntington's disease gene therapy drug, AMT-130, and its Biologics License Application (BLA) timeline. Investors have until April 13, 2026, to seek lead plaintiff status.

feed.title: Diamondback Energy Insiders Sold Shares Worth Over $1.7M
feed.summary: Two Diamondback Energy insiders, Teresa L. Dick and Matt Zmigrosky, recently sold shares totaling over $1.7 million. Dick, the CAO, Executive Vice President, and Assistant Secretary, sold $950,000 worth of shares at $190.00 each, while Zmigrosky, EVP and Chief Legal and Administrative Officer, sold shares worth $781,292 at a weighted average price of $190.5128. Following these transactions, both individuals still retain significant ownership in the company.

feed.title: Axogen chief innovation officer DeVinney sells $171k in shares
feed.summary: Axogen's Chief Innovation Officer, Erick Wayne DeVinney, sold 5,221 shares of common stock for approximately $171,457 and disposed of 2,870 shares to cover tax obligations. Despite the stock's recent retreat, analysts maintain a bullish outlook with price targets between $39 and $43. The company also recently surpassed Q4 2025 earnings and revenue expectations.

feed.title: Ma Botao details 16.8M Class B stake in Zhibao (NASDAQ: ZBAO)
feed.summary: Zhibao Technology Inc.'s CEO and director, Ma Botao, has filed an initial statement of beneficial ownership (Form 3) disclosing an indirect stake of 16,816,692 Class B ordinary shares. These shares are held through various entities including Mavy Holdings Limited, Shenbao Limited Partnership, Shanghai Xinhui Investment Consulting Co., Ltd., and Maximum Global Holdings Limited. Ma Botao is considered a major shareholder, with voting and dispositive power over these holdings.

feed.title: Ryerson Holding Corp ($RYZ) CEO 2025 Pay Revealed
feed.summary: Quiver Quantitative estimates that Edward J. Lehner, CEO of Ryerson Holding Corp ($RYZ), received approximately $17,857,908 in compensation in 2025. This estimate is based on a DEF14A filing submitted to the SEC on March 18, 2026. The article advises readers that these are estimates and not financial advice, and inaccuracies may occur.

feed.title: Alliant Energy (NASDAQ: LNT) launches $1B ATM shelf with forward-sale option
feed.summary: Alliant Energy Corporation has filed a prospectus supplement to offer up to $1 billion of common stock through an "at-the-market" distribution agreement, valid until December 31, 2029. This offering allows sales through designated agents or via forward sale agreements with specified forward purchasers. Proceeds from the offering will be primarily used for general corporate purposes, including debt repayment and capital expenditures, with specific mechanisms for how and when proceeds from various settlement options will be received.

feed.title: Huntington National Bank review (2026): A regional bank recognized as leader in climate responsibility
feed.summary: Huntington National Bank is a regional bank, established in 1866 and headquartered in Columbus, OH, operating primarily in 14 U.S. states with 1,000 branches and over 1,600 ATMs. The bank offers a variety of checking, savings, credit card, and loan products, many with fee waiver opportunities and benefits like early direct deposit and overdraft flexibility. Huntington is also recognized for its leadership in climate responsibility and community involvement, having received awards for promoting homeownership and being acknowledged by Bank.Green.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price Names New Head of Global Retirement Team
feed.summary: Jessica Sclafani has been named the new head of T. Rowe Price Group Inc.’s retirement strategist team, succeeding Michael Davis, who is retiring on May 1. Sclafani joined T. Rowe Price in 2022 and will lead the expanded global retirement team leadership, which also includes Richard Parkin for U.K. retirement and Scott Keller for Americas, APAC, and EMEA distribution. Davis, who joined in 2016 and led the global retirement strategy team, is celebrated for his significant contributions to the firm's retirement business.

feed.title: BOSTON SCIENTIFIC CORP ($BSX) CEO 2025 Pay Revealed
feed.summary: Boston Scientific Corp's CEO, Michael F. Mahoney, is estimated to have received $23,532,050 in compensation for 2025, an 8.97% increase from 2024, based on a recent SEC filing. The article also details insider trading activity, with multiple executives selling shares, and lists government contracts awarded to BSX. Additionally, it provides information on congressional stock trading involving BSX, hedge fund activity, analyst ratings, and price targets.

feed.title: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc. ($GEHC) President and CEO 2025 Pay Revealed
feed.summary: GE HealthCare Technologies Inc.'s President and CEO, Peter J. Arduini, is estimated to receive $18,562,794 in compensation for 2025, a 4.98% decrease from 2024. This estimate is based on a recent SEC DEF14A filing. The article also details recent hedge fund activity, with 541 institutional investors adding shares and 687 decreasing positions, and analyst ratings which are predominantly "Buy" or "Overweight" with a median price target of $92.0 from 11 analysts.

feed.title: Pinnacle Financial (PNFP) Joins Prestigious Bank Index
feed.summary: Pinnacle Financial (PNFP) is being added to the KBW Nasdaq Bank Index, signifying its strong growth and strong performance in the financial sector. The company, operating through Pinnacle Bank, boasts consistent revenue growth, efficient operations, and an attractive valuation with its P/E ratio near a 2-year low. Despite some inherent sector risks, its robust financial health, confirmed by a high Piotroski F-Score and positive insider buying trends, indicates a promising outlook.

feed.title: Alliant Energy (NASDAQ: LNT) launches $1B at-the-market and forward equity program
feed.summary: Alliant Energy (NASDAQ: LNT) has initiated a new equity distribution agreement, allowing it to sell up to $1 billion in common stock through at-the-market offerings and forward sale arrangements. The proceeds are intended for general corporate purposes, including debt repayment, working capital, and capital expenditures. This program provides the company with flexibility in financing its investments as a regulated utility.
